attributes.description: Alerts when `envoy.server.memory_allocated / memory_heap_size > 0.9`. Sustained high ratios indicate memory pressure or a leak and risk OOM kills.
attributes.name: [Envoy OTel] High memory pressure

investigation_guide.blob:
## Envoy high memory pressure

### What fired
`memory_allocated / memory_heap_size` exceeded 0.9 over the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
Envoy is C++ and uses tcmalloc-style allocators. High allocated/heap ratios usually mean too many active connections, huge header sets, or a genuine leak. OOM kills the process entirely.

### Triage
1. Check concurrent connection count and typical response size.
2. Look at `envoy.server.live` / restart counters to see if the process has been flapping.
3. Compare with recent traffic growth or config pushes.

### Remediation
- Increase pod memory limits.
- Tune `overload_manager` to trigger backpressure before OOM.
- Collect a heap profile if a leak is suspected.

### Tuning
- 0.9 threshold balances noise and severity; lower to 0.85 for tighter environments.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-statsdreceiver-default
| WHERE data_stream.dataset == "statsdreceiver"
| WHERE envoy.server.memory_allocated IS NOT NULL
    AND envoy.server.memory_heap_size IS NOT NULL
    AND envoy.server.memory_heap_size > 0
// Ratio of allocated to heap — sustained high values indicate pressure or leak
| EVAL mem_ratio = envoy.server.memory_allocated / envoy.server.memory_heap_size
| STATS max_ratio = MAX(mem_ratio)
// Adjust threshold (0.9 = 90%) based on your memory headroom expectations
| WHERE max_ratio > 0.9
